tiktok.title: TikTok Brand Deal Rate Calculator 2026 | SponsorJuice
tiktok.description: Calculate how much TikTok creators should charge for brand deals. Free rate calculator with deliverables basket and pitch template generator.
tiktok.intro: How much should a TikTok creator charge for sponsored content? Use our free calculator to estimate brand deal rates based on views, niche, audience location, and deliverables. TikTok sponsorship pricing depends on average views per post more than follower count — brands pay for reach. Adjust the sliders below to match metrics, add deliverables to a package, and copy a ready-made pitch email.
tiktok.howToUse: Enter average views per TikTok post and select your content niche. Set your primary audience location — creators with US and Canadian audiences typically command higher sponsorship rates. Add deliverables like dedicated videos, integrated mentions, Stories, and bio link placements to build a package. The calculator shows a low-to-high rate range you can use in negotiations, then save results or copy the auto-generated pitch email.
tiktok.crossLinkSuffix: for TikTok outreach tips, then compare your quote against our Instagram and YouTube calculators if you publish on multiple platforms.
tiktok.howToUseHeading: How do I calculate TikTok sponsorship rates?
tiktok.citabilityBlock: A TikTok creator sponsorship rate is the price a brand pays for sponsored content on TikTok, typically calculated per view rather than per follower. SponsorJuice estimates TikTok brand deal rates using average views per post multiplied by a platform coefficient of $0.005 to $0.012 per view, then adjusted for content niche and audience geography. For example, a beauty creator with 10,000 average views and a US/Canada audience might charge $60 to $144 for a dedicated video. Finance and tech niches command higher multipliers than comedy. Industry benchmarks from influencer marketing reports suggest views consistently outweigh raw follower counts when brands set budgets. Bundle deliverables like Stories and bio links to increase package value, then use the interactive calculator below to model your own metrics.

faqs.question: How much should I charge for a TikTok brand deal?
faqs.answer: TikTok brand deal rates typically range from $0.005 to $0.012 per view, multiplied by niche and audience geography. A creator with 50K followers and 10K average views in the beauty niche targeting US audiences might charge $60–$144 per dedicated video.

faqs.question: What factors affect TikTok sponsorship rates?
faqs.answer: Key factors include average views per post, content niche (finance and tech pay more than comedy), audience geography (US/CA audiences command premium rates), and deliverable type (dedicated videos vs. integrated ads vs. bio links).

faqs.question: Do TikTok follower count or views matter more for pricing?
faqs.answer: Views matter more than follower count for pricing. Brands care about actual reach and engagement, which is reflected in average views per post rather than total follower count.

instagram.title: Instagram Sponsorship Rate Calculator 2026 | SponsorJuice
instagram.description: Calculate Instagram influencer rates for brand deals. Free calculator with package pricing, deliverables basket, and outreach pitch generator.
instagram.intro: How much should an Instagram influencer charge for sponsored posts? Estimate creator rates based on views, niche, audience location, and the deliverables in a package. Instagram Reels, feed posts, and Stories each carry different value — our calculator lets creators bundle them into a single sponsorship price. Finance and tech niches typically command higher rates than comedy or lifestyle content.
instagram.howToUse: Start with average Reels or feed post views and your content niche. Choose audience geography — US and Canadian followers often justify premium Instagram rates. Bundle Reels, Stories, feed posts, and bio links into one package price. Use the rate range to anchor negotiations, save your quote locally, and copy the outreach pitch when you are ready to email brands.
instagram.crossLinkSuffix: to structure your Instagram outreach, then cross-check rates on our TikTok and YouTube calculators if you repurpose content across channels.
instagram.howToUseHeading: How do I calculate Instagram sponsorship rates?
instagram.citabilityBlock: An Instagram sponsorship rate is what an influencer charges brands for sponsored Reels, feed posts, Stories, or bundled packages. SponsorJuice uses average post or Reel views multiplied by an Instagram coefficient of $0.007 to $0.015 per view, scaled by niche and audience geography. A tech creator with 10,000 average views and a US/Canada audience might charge $105 to $225 per dedicated post. Reels often match or exceed static feed post rates because of higher reach potential. Pricing data aligns with publicly cited CPM ranges from influencer marketing platforms and agency rate cards. Package multiple deliverables together rather than pricing each post in isolation — the calculator below builds custom package rates and generates outreach copy.

faqs.question: How much should I charge for an Instagram sponsorship?
faqs.answer: Instagram sponsorship rates typically range from $0.007 to $0.015 per view. A creator with 50K followers, 10K average views in the tech niche targeting US audiences might charge $105–$225 per dedicated post.

faqs.question: What deliverables should I include in an Instagram brand deal?
faqs.answer: Common deliverables include dedicated feed posts, Stories, Reels, integrated product mentions, and bio link placements. Each adds value — use our deliverables basket to build custom package rates.

faqs.question: How do Instagram Reels rates compare to feed posts?
faqs.answer: Reels typically command similar or slightly higher rates than static feed posts due to higher reach potential. Package multiple deliverables together for better overall compensation.

youtube.title: YouTube Sponsorship Rate Calculator 2026 | SponsorJuice
youtube.description: Calculate YouTube creator sponsorship rates for brand deals. Free rate calculator with CPM-based pricing, deliverables, and pitch templates.
youtube.intro: How much should a YouTuber charge for sponsored content? Calculate brand deal rates based on average views, niche, audience geography, and deliverable packages. YouTube sponsorships typically pay more per view than TikTok or Instagram due to longer watch times and higher production value. Use dedicated video, integrated ad, and cross-platform deliverable options to build a quote.
youtube.howToUse: Enter average long-form video views (or Shorts views if that is your primary format) and select your niche. US and Canadian watch time typically commands higher CPMs than global averages. Price dedicated videos, integrated segments, and cross-platform mentions separately or as a bundle. Save quotes locally and send brands the generated pitch email when your range looks right.
youtube.crossLinkSuffix: for YouTube sponsorship emails, then explore our TikTok and Instagram calculators if you distribute clips or cross-post to short-form platforms.
youtube.howToUseHeading: How do I calculate YouTube sponsorship rates?
youtube.citabilityBlock: A YouTube sponsorship rate is the fee a creator charges for brand integrations in long-form videos, Shorts, or dedicated sponsor videos. SponsorJuice applies a YouTube coefficient of $0.015 to $0.035 per average view, multiplied by niche and geography weights. A finance creator averaging 50,000 views with a US/Canada audience might charge $1,350 to $3,150 for a dedicated video. Integrated segments typically price at roughly 60% of a dedicated video rate. Long-form sponsorships generally command higher fees than Shorts due to watch time and production value. Estimates draw on industry CPM benchmarks cited in creator economy research. Use the calculator below to price dedicated videos, integrated mentions, and cross-platform deliverables in one quote.

faqs.question: How much should I charge for a YouTube sponsorship?
faqs.answer: YouTube sponsorship rates typically range from $0.015 to $0.035 per view. A creator with 100K subscribers, 50K average views in the finance niche targeting US audiences might charge $1,350–$3,150 per dedicated video.

faqs.question: What is the difference between integrated and dedicated YouTube ads?
faqs.answer: A dedicated video is entirely about the sponsor's product (highest rate). An integrated ad is a segment within regular content (typically 60% of dedicated rate). Use the deliverables basket to price each type.

faqs.question: Do YouTube Shorts pay less than long-form sponsorships?
faqs.answer: Yes, YouTube Shorts typically command lower sponsorship rates than long-form videos due to shorter watch time and different ad integration formats. Long-form dedicated videos remain the highest-value deliverable.
